verses.latin_vulgate: In finem, psalmus David.   Domine, probasti me, et cognovisti me ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לַ֭מְנַצֵּחַ לְדָוִ֣ד מִזְמ֑וֹר יְהֹוָ֥ה חֲ֝קַרְתַּ֗נִי וַתֵּדָֽע׃
verses.king_james: O Lord, thou hast searched me, and known me.

verses.latin_vulgate: tu cognovisti sessionem meam et resurrectionem meam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַתָּ֣ה יָ֭דַעְתָּ שִׁבְתִּ֣י וְקוּמִ֑י בַּ֥נְתָּה לְ֝רֵעִ֗י מֵרָחֽוֹק׃
verses.king_james: Thou knowest my downsitting and mine uprising, thou understandest my thought afar off.

verses.latin_vulgate: Intellexisti cogitationes meas de longe ;  semitam meam et funiculum meum investigasti :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אׇרְחִ֣י וְרִבְעִ֣י זֵרִ֑יתָ וְֽכׇל־דְּרָכַ֥י הִסְכַּֽנְתָּה׃
verses.king_james: Thou compassest my path and my lying down, and art acquainted with all my ways.

verses.latin_vulgate: et omnes vias meas prævidisti,  quia non est sermo in lingua mea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י אֵ֣ין מִ֭לָּה בִּלְשׁוֹנִ֑י הֵ֥ן יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה יָדַ֥עְתָּ כֻלָּֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: For there is not a word in my tongue, but, lo, O Lord, thou knowest it altogether.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce, Domine, tu cognovisti omnia,  novissima et antiqua.  Tu formasti me, et posuisti super me manum tuam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָח֣וֹר וָקֶ֣דֶם צַרְתָּ֑נִי וַתָּ֖שֶׁת עָלַ֣י כַּפֶּֽכָה׃
verses.king_james: Thou hast beset me behind and before, and laid thine hand upon me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mirabilis facta est scientia tua ex me ;  confortata est, et non potero ad eam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: (פלאיה) [פְּלִ֣יאָֽה] דַ֣עַת מִמֶּ֑נִּי נִ֝שְׂגְּבָ֗ה לֹא־א֥וּכַֽל לָֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: Such knowledge is too wonderful for me; it is high, I cannot attain unto it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quo ibo a spiritu tuo ?  et quo a facie tua fugiam ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָ֭נָֽה אֵלֵ֣ךְ מֵרוּחֶ֑ךָ וְ֝אָ֗נָה מִפָּנֶ֥יךָ אֶבְרָֽח׃
verses.king_james: Whither shall I go from thy spirit? or whither shall I flee from thy presence?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si ascendero in cælum, tu illic es ;  si descendero in infernum, ades.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־אֶסַּ֣ק שָׁ֭מַיִם שָׁ֣ם אָ֑תָּה וְאַצִּ֖יעָה שְּׁא֣וֹל הִנֶּֽךָּ׃
verses.king_james: If I ascend up into heaven, thou art there: if I make my bed in hell, behold, thou art there.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si sumpsero pennas meas diluculo,  et habitavero in extremis maris,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶשָּׂ֥א כַנְפֵי־שָׁ֑חַר אֶ֝שְׁכְּנָ֗ה בְּאַחֲרִ֥ית יָֽם׃
verses.king_james: If I take the wings of the morning, and dwell in the uttermost parts of the sea;

verses.latin_vulgate: etenim illuc manus tua deducet me,  et tenebit me dextera tua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־שָׁ֭ם יָדְךָ֣ תַנְחֵ֑נִי וְֽתֹאחֲזֵ֥נִי יְמִינֶֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: Even there shall thy hand lead me, and thy right hand shall hold me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixi : Forsitan tenebræ conculcabunt me ;  et nox illuminatio mea in deliciis meis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָ֭אֹמַר אַךְ־חֹ֣שֶׁךְ יְשׁוּפֵ֑נִי וְ֝לַ֗יְלָה א֣וֹר בַּעֲדֵֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: If I say, Surely the darkness shall cover me; even the night shall be light about me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia tenebræ non obscurabuntur a te,  et nox sicut dies illuminabitur :  sicut tenebræ ejus, ita et lumen ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־חֹשֶׁךְ֮ לֹא־יַחְשִׁ֢יךְ מִ֫מֶּ֥ךָּ וְ֭לַיְלָה כַּיּ֣וֹם יָאִ֑יר כַּ֝חֲשֵׁיכָ֗ה כָּאוֹרָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Yea, the darkness hideth not from thee; but the night shineth as the day: the darkness and the light are both alike to thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia tu possedisti renes meos ;  suscepisti me de utero matris meæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־אַ֭תָּה קָנִ֣יתָ כִלְיֹתָ֑י תְּ֝סֻכֵּ֗נִי בְּבֶ֣טֶן אִמִּֽי׃
verses.king_james: For thou hast possessed my reins: thou hast covered me in my mother’s womb.

verses.latin_vulgate: Confitebor tibi, quia terribiliter magnificatus es ;  mirabilia opera tua, et anima mea cognoscit nimis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אוֹדְךָ֗ עַ֤ל כִּ֥י נוֹרָא֗וֹת נִ֫פְלֵ֥יתִי נִפְלָאִ֥ים מַעֲשֶׂ֑יךָ וְ֝נַפְשִׁ֗י יֹדַ֥עַת מְאֹֽד׃
verses.king_james: I will praise thee; for I am fearfully and wonderfully made: marvellous are thy works; and that my soul knoweth right well.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non est occultatum os meum a te, quod fecisti in occulto ;  et substantia mea in inferioribus terræ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹֽא־נִכְחַ֥ד עׇצְמִ֗י מִ֫מֶּ֥ךָּ אֲשֶׁר־עֻשֵּׂ֥יתִי בַסֵּ֑תֶר רֻ֝קַּ֗מְתִּי בְּֽתַחְתִּיּ֥וֹת אָֽרֶץ׃
verses.king_james: My substance was not hid from thee, when I was made in secret, and curiously wrought in the lowest parts of the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Imperfectum meum viderunt oculi tui,  et in libro tuo omnes scribentur.  Dies formabuntur, et nemo in eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גׇּלְמִ֤י ׀ רָ֘א֤וּ עֵינֶ֗יךָ וְעַֽל־סִפְרְךָ֮ כֻּלָּ֢ם יִכָּ֫תֵ֥בוּ יָמִ֥ים יֻצָּ֑רוּ (ולא) [וְל֖וֹ] אֶחָ֣ד בָּהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: Thine eyes did see my substance, yet being unperfect; and in thy book all my members were written, which in continuance were fashioned, when as yet there was none of them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mihi autem nimis honorificati sunt amici tui, Deus ;  nimis confortatus est principatus eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלִ֗י מַה־יָּקְר֣וּ רֵעֶ֣יךָ אֵ֑ל מֶ֥ה עָ֝צְמ֗וּ רָאשֵׁיהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: How precious also are thy thoughts unto me, O God! how great is the sum of them!

verses.latin_vulgate: Dinumerabo eos, et super arenam multiplicabuntur.  Exsurrexi, et adhuc sum tecum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶ֭סְפְּרֵם מֵח֣וֹל יִרְבּ֑וּן הֱ֝קִיצֹ֗תִי וְעוֹדִ֥י עִמָּֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: If I should count them, they are more in number than the sand: when I awake, I am still with thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si occideris, Deus, peccatores,  viri sanguinum, declinate a me :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־תִּקְטֹ֖ל אֱל֥וֹהַּ ׀ רָשָׁ֑ע וְאַנְשֵׁ֥י דָ֝מִ֗ים ס֣וּרוּ מֶֽנִּי׃
verses.king_james: Surely thou wilt slay the wicked, O God: depart from me therefore, ye bloody men.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia dicitis in cogitatione :  Accipient in vanitate civitates tuas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲשֶׁ֣ר יֹ֭מְרוּךָ לִמְזִמָּ֑ה נָשׂ֖וּא לַשָּׁ֣וְא עָרֶֽיךָ׃
verses.king_james: For they speak against thee wickedly, and thine enemies take thy name in vain.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nonne qui oderunt te, Domine, oderam,  et super inimicos tuos tabescebam ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲלֽוֹא־מְשַׂנְאֶ֖יךָ יְהֹוָ֥ה ׀ אֶשְׂנָ֑א וּ֝בִתְקוֹמְמֶ֗יךָ אֶתְקוֹטָֽט׃
verses.king_james: Do not I hate them, O Lord, that hate thee? and am not I grieved with those that rise up against thee?

verses.latin_vulgate: Perfecto odio oderam illos,  et inimici facti sunt mihi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תַּכְלִ֣ית שִׂנְאָ֣ה שְׂנֵאתִ֑ים לְ֝אוֹיְבִ֗ים הָ֣יוּ לִֽי׃
verses.king_james: I hate them with perfect hatred: I count them mine enemies.

verses.latin_vulgate: Proba me, Deus, et scito cor meum ;  interroga me, et cognosce semitas meas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חׇקְרֵ֣נִי אֵ֭ל וְדַ֣ע לְבָבִ֑י בְּ֝חָנֵ֗נִי וְדַ֣ע שַׂרְעַפָּֽי׃
verses.king_james: Search me, O God, and know my heart: try me, and know my thoughts:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et vide si via iniquitatis in me est,  et deduc me in via æterna.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּרְאֵ֗ה אִם־דֶּֽרֶךְ־עֹ֥צֶב בִּ֑י וּ֝נְחֵ֗נִי בְּדֶ֣רֶךְ עוֹלָֽם׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: And see if there be any wicked way in me, and lead me in the way everlasting.
